footer.tagline: Eternal abode of divine love. A free, centralized index of ISKCON lectures, kirtans and festivals from official channels.
footer.disclaimer: Goloka is an index, not a host — every video plays through the official YouTube player and links back to its source channel. All content belongs to its creators.

calendarStrip.inDays: In {days, plural, one {# day} other {# days}}

aarti.shayanaHint: a quiet evening of the holy name

emptyState.default: Nothing here yet — like Vrindavan before the festival. Check back soon.
emptyState.tryAnotherSearch: Nothing here yet — like Vrindavan before the festival. Try another search.
emptyState.noFilterMatch: No videos match these filters yet — try a different one.
emptyState.libraryEmpty: Your library is empty — the heart and bookmark on any video fill it.
emptyState.noFavourites: Nothing favourited yet — tap the heart on any video.
emptyState.noWatchLater: Nothing saved for later yet — tap the bookmark on any video.
emptyState.collectionGone: These videos are no longer available — the devotee who shared this list, or the videos themselves, may have moved on.
emptyState.notFoundMessage: This page wandered off the path — nothing to see here.
emptyState.offlineMessage: Goloka needs a connection to fetch videos — reconnect and try again.
emptyState.notIndexedMessage: This video isn't in Goloka's index — we index a curated set of official ISKCON channels. You can still watch it on YouTube, or explore what's here.

push.title: Notifications
push.description: Anonymous and opt-in — no account, no tracking, unsubscribe anytime.
push.blocked: Notifications are blocked for this site in your browser settings — allow them there first.
push.unsupported: Notifications aren't available on this browser — try Chrome or Firefox, or add Goloka to your home screen first.
push.festivalsHint: One quiet ping on ekādaśī mornings.
push.liveHint: The moment a temple goes live.

library.title: My Library
library.signInPrompt: Sign in to keep favourites and a watch-later list that follow you across devices.
library.privacyNote: Your account stores only these two lists — what you watch is never sent to the server, signed in or not. Deleting your account deletes everything.
library.createFailed: Couldn't create the link — try again.

sadhana.title: My Sādhana
sadhana.signInPrompt: Sign in to keep a quiet record of the rounds you chant — held only for you, on every device.
sadhana.privacyNote: This record is yours alone — never shared, never compared, never sent anywhere but your own account. Deleting your account deletes it completely.
sadhana.intro: A quiet record of the holy name in your days — yours alone, held gently, never a measure of your worth.
sadhana.emptyMonth: No rounds recorded this month yet — whenever you chant while signed in, they will rest here quietly.
sadhana.footNote: This is the only record Goloka keeps of your chanting, kept only because you signed in, and yours to delete with your account at any time. Chanting while signed out stays on your device alone, as it always has.

about.intro: Goloka is a free, ad-free index of ISKCON content on YouTube — lectures, kirtans, festivals, and documentaries from dozens of temples and teachers, gathered in one calm place instead of scattered across the platform.
about.indexBody: Goloka stores no video files. Every listing is metadata — a title, a thumbnail, a category — and every video plays through YouTube's own standard embedded player. Watching here is the same as watching on YouTube directly; Goloka just makes it easier to find.
about.belongsTitle: All content belongs to its creators
about.belongsBody: Every video links back to its source channel. Goloka doesn't re-host, re-edit, or monetize anyone's content — it's simply a directory pointing to work that already belongs to the temples and speakers who made it.
about.privacyBody1: Goloka never tracks what you watch. "Continue Watching" lives only in your own browser and is never sent anywhere — clearing your browser data clears it completely, signed in or not. Chanting while signed out is the same: your rounds stay on your device alone and reset each day, kept nowhere else.
about.privacyBody2: Signing in is optional and exists for two quiet things: keeping your favourites and watch-later list with you across devices, and — if you chant while signed in — keeping your own record of the rounds you complete, by day, month and year. That's all an account holds: two lists of video links, and your own japa record. It is yours alone, never shared or compared with anyone. No viewing history, no analytics profile, nothing sold or shared. Delete your account and everything it held — lists and japa record alike — is deleted with it.
about.independentBody: Goloka is an independent, non-commercial project — not an official ISKCON website, and not affiliated with any single temple. It runs entirely on free-tier infrastructure, with no ads and nothing for sale.
about.helpBody: Know a channel that belongs here, or spot something miscategorized? <sendNote>Send a note</sendNote> — every suggestion is read.

books.intro: Śrīla Prabhupāda's books — the heart of everything taught in the lectures indexed here. Read them free on the BBT's own Vedabase, or get a printed copy from the official store.
books.essentialsSubtitle: From the official BBT store only — Goloka links, never sells.
books.disclaimer: All books and goods are published and sold by the Bhaktivedanta Book Trust and official ISKCON outlets. Goloka is an index — it hosts nothing and earns nothing from these links.

leaders.intro: ISKCON teachers and speakers whose talks, kirtans and classes are indexed on Goloka.

start.welcomeP1: You don't need to believe anything to be here. You don't need to know a word of Sanskrit, or have read a single page of scripture, or have ever set foot inside a temple. None of that was ever the door - you're already standing inside it.
start.welcomeP2: Think of this simply as an invitation: to sit for a few quiet minutes and meet someone named Kṛṣṇa, through the eyes of people who love him very much. There is nothing to sign, nothing to join, no test waiting at the end. Watch if you'd like. Pause whenever you'd like. Come back whenever you'd like - or don't, and know you were welcome all the same.
start.welcomeP3: Below are ten small questions, each answered gently by a short video, one leading softly to the next. You don't have to take them in order, and you don't have to take them all. If all you ever watch is the first one, you will still have been received here exactly as you are. Let's begin gently.
start.videoUnavailable: This video isn't available right now - check back soon.
start.outroBody: The mahā-mantra itself is only a breath away - visit <chant>the chant</chant> and say the holy names for yourself. Or go deeper into the <books>Sacred Library</books>, where the Bhagavad-gītā waits as a natural next read. Or find a <temples>temple</temples> near you, and see one of these evenings with your own eyes.
start.outroClose: And whenever you're ready - simply begin.

temples.intro: Major ISKCON centers around the world. Every card links to the temple's own website; temples whose channels Goloka indexes link to their catalog here too.
temples.knowTemple: Know a temple that belongs here? <sendNote>Send a note</sendNote>.

chant.resetConfirmAria: Tap again to confirm resetting today's count
chant.soundAria: Play a soft tone when a round completes
chant.voiceAria: Turn on hands-free chanting — listens on this device only, never recorded or sent anywhere
chant.voiceNudgeMinusAria: Correct the count: remove one bead
chant.voiceNudgePlusAria: Correct the count: add one bead
chant.voicePrivacy: Listens only on your device and never records or sends your voice. It's a gentle aid and may occasionally miscount — nudge a bead above if it slips.
chant.voiceMicDenied: Microphone access wasn't granted — tap to count instead, eyes closed.
chant.voiceUnsupported: Hands-free chanting isn't available in this browser — tap to count instead.

calendar.intro: The sacred year at a glance — ekādaśīs, and the appearance and disappearance days of the Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava ācāryas, precisely dated for Māyāpur.

_note: Source-of-truth English strings for the site chrome. hi/bn/ru/es are machine-translated from this file.

homeDoorways.chantHint: A still place to call out to Him
